top_categories.workout_context: 630 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 630 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Suisse Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
630 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Tour de Suisse Challenge's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Tour de Suisse Challenge's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 630 miles.
Tempo is Tour de Suisse Challenge's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 630 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
Tour de Suisse Challenge's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Suisse Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 630 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 630 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 630 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Tour de Suisse Challenge demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Endurance to prepare you for 630 miles of mixed terrain in Various, Switzerland.
